meta.subject: Hospitality Management
meta.philosophy: The Hospitality Management (HM) Syllabus has been developed by the need to provide a national curriculum for Zambia that is aligned with the competence based curriculum and 21st Century Skills. It aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that are aimed at enabling children to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society. The content/topics provided in this syllabi are aimed at facilitating holistic development of learners' creativity, initiative, problem solving, physical, mental, emotional skills among others. The suggested activities are designed to offer learners' hands-on experiences through manipulation of real objects, interaction with nature and learning through practice.
meta.competence_learning: Integrating STEM topics into hospitality management lessons will help the learners become more engaging and relevant. Science, Technology, Engineering and Math is becoming increasingly important in the hospitality industry. The use of technology is a key component in hospitality operations and data analysis for decision making. Hospitality managers utilize STEM skills to streamline processes, improve efficiency, and enhance the guest experience.
goals: Evaluate the effects of the hospitality industry on the economy.
Apply skills to deliver exceptional front office services.
Make use of records in the housekeeping department.
Perform laundry procedures in hospitality facility.
Carry out the basic sequence in Food and Beverage services.
Show skillful serving of food and beverages.
Employ various food commodities in cookery.
Apply hair dye and colouring techniques.

specific_outcomes: Evaluate the effects of the hospitality industry on the economy

Discuss the effects of hospitality industry on the economy: Local economic development, Small and medium enterprise, Job creation and entrepreneurship (Chef, receptionist, housekeeper), Foreign exchange earnings, Infrastructure development (transportation, amenities, hotels, attractions), Tourism growth (increased demand, tourist facilities)
Undertake an education tour to appreciate the benefits of hospitality industry on the economy
Apply skills to deliver exceptional front office services

Creating a mock scenario of front office desk customer service: Pre-arrival stage (inquiries, reservations), Arrivals stage (Porter, Registration, room assignment), Occupancy stage (guest services, billing, complaints), Departure stage (payments, handover)
Social skills (Posture, Eye contact, Poise and deportment, Gestures)
Make use of records in the housekeeping department
Documenting housekeeping records (Attendance book, handing over report book, Housekeeper's room report, Lost and found book, Room checklist, Theft complaint book, Gift record book)
Organizing records in housekeeping department
Conducting a mock audit of housekeeping records
Apply correct cleaning processes
Identifying cleaning areas and rooms (Corridors, public areas, conference rooms)
Using correct cleaning procedures for different areas (Sanitizing, Sterilizing, disinfecting, decontaminating, polishing, mopping, vacuuming)
Making use of cleaning products on cars, carpets, upholstery, furniture
Perform laundry procedures in hospitality facility
Demonstrating laundry procedures for guest linen (collecting, sorting, pre-treatment, washing, drying, ironing, storage, distribution)
Conduct routine housekeeping maintenance
Conducting routine maintenance (Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-annually and annually)
Carrying out preventive maintenance (clean and sanitize guest rooms, check and replace batteries, test emergency lighting, inspect and clean laundry facilities and surroundings)
Create different menus
Discuss functions of the menu / menu card
Identify types of menus (Table d'hôte, à la carte, Carte du jour, Cycle, Static menu, Health menu, Bar menu)
Discuss menu planning considerations (Seasonality, Dietary requirements, Preparation, Variety, Ethnic considerations)
Explore menu design elements (Attractive, Clean, Compliment occasion, Easy to read)
Analyze menu content elements (Language, Accuracy, Sales mix)
Creating a menu and menu card for a function of your choice
Cater for different functions
Discuss types of functions (business, national)
Coordinating event catering (event décor, security, ushering, equipment and utensils supply)
Preparing indigenous foods for a ceremony from any province (Zambian: matebeto, sikenge, nkolo, lapoly, acizyo le)
Carry out the basic sequence in Food and Beverage services
Arrange the pre-requirements for basic sequence of service (food equipment, beverage service equipment, billing requirements)
Illustrate the basic sequence of service: Pre-service (greeting, seating, present menu), Service (order, serve food, clear table), Post-service (present bill, process payment, farewell)
Show skillful serving of food and beverages
Demonstrating skillful serving of beverages (pre-preparation, mise en place, cleaning the environment, reshaping/cleaning the equipment, arrange station)
Displaying table setting skills (Informal, Linen, Glassware, Cutlery/flatware)
Practising the serving of food in different styles of service (Plated, assisted service, self-service)
Practising beverage service (Alcoholic beverages - beers, spirits, wines; Non-alcoholic beverages - refreshing, nourishing, stimulating; Clearing, table crumbing, down, resetting the table)
Simulating a themed event for a banquet, a casual brunch, a cocktail reception
Cost food and beverage
Costing food and beverages (planning, recipe development and dish costing)
Employ various food commodities in cookery

Classifying food commodities: Animal foods (meats, poultry, fish, lard); Plant foods (vegetables, fruits, pulses); Food of animal origin (dairy products including eggs, fats, cooking oil, edible fats such as lard); Condiments (pepper, tabasco sauce, soy sauce, shire sauce); Sugar and syrups (granulated, castor, icing, demerara); Beverages (alcoholic and non-alcoholic); Groceries, Danish pastries, croissants
Carrying out an experiment to determine the effect of heat and chemicals on nutrients in food (proteins, carbohydrates, fats)
Developing profiling recipes and documenting Zambian indigenous recipes according to provinces
Care of hair and scalp
Explaining hair anatomy and analysis (hair shaft, root, hair bulb, dermatological papilla, cuticle)
Discussing hair properties (texture, porosity, elasticity, tensile strength, moisture content, pH level)
Identifying hair and scalp problems (dandruff, hair fall, damaged hair, oily scalp, chemically treated hair)
Practising hair care (shampooing, plaiting hair, barbing hair, locking)
Apply hair dye and colouring techniques
Differentiating hair colour and product (brand, price, features)
Applying hair colour (prepare, choose the right amount and application, specific sections, work the product as desired)
Streaking hair (horizontal, vertical, diagonal, balayage)
Discussing advantages and disadvantages of streaking (dimension and texture, brightening looks)
Set up a business venture
Exploring different types of businesses (partnership, sole trade, limited business)
Explaining the requirements for a business (pre-operational requirements, business plan, registration and financial)
Operational requirements (business location)
Regulatory compliance (health and safety)
